_doc:
Per Codex 2026-06-14 F1 [high]: 20 (split, diagnoser, method, case) tuples whose diagnosis rows were removed by the 2026-06-09 / 2026-06-10 cleanup. These rows had non-allowlisted provider_error prefixes (claude-CLI RuntimeError on v1, JSONDecodeError on v2, post_api_error on v3). The cleanup removed them so the validate_committed_diagnosis_provider_errors.py release check could pass, BUT removing rows shrinks the eval denominator and inflates macro means.

The diagnosis-vs-context release check (validate_diagnosis_vs_context_consistency.py) treats these tuples as explicitly-excluded missing cases. Future re-runs can re-introduce them under the 2026-06-12 F1 method-level skip discipline (the runner now refuses to persist non-allowlisted provider_error rows, so a fresh re-run that hits the same transient failure would NOT silently overwrite — it would log FAIL_PROVIDER_ERROR and preserve any prior valid artifacts).

When a future re-run successfully produces a clean (non-provider-error) row for one of these tuples, drop the corresponding entry from this list. The release check will then require a present diagnosis row for that case.
_codex_finding: Codex 2026-06-14 F1 [high]: failed-diagnoser rows deleted instead of counted; the eval denominator shrinks → score inflation. Recommendation: add an explicit exclusion manifest and a release check that compares diagnosis case IDs against the source context manifest. This file IS that exclusion manifest.
